violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Staff member has taken class and must schedule exam. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026
Food debris was observed in the hand-washing sink in the prep area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/09/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Pork was observed cooling on the counter in a container greater than four inches deep. Staff spread out on a sheet pan and placed in walk-in. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/09/2026

Staff were not knowledgeable of 3-bay sink set-up. The person in charge must demonstrate to the health department knowledge of foodborne disease prevention, application of HACCP principles, and the requirements of this Code. Inspector, PIC, and staff set-up 3-bay sink together. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Staff member has taken class and must schedule exam. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided RIDOH guidance. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized; ice disposed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026

Chicken and rice, prepared in advance, were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

Working containers of various spices and sauces are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
A bowl of chicken (raw, in process) was observed stacked on top of a garbage can. Staff removed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026
Cases of beverages and fryer oil were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen hallway. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026
Establishment was using an unapproved area for food preparation (back storeroom). PIC had staff move to an area of the kitchen. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

Utensils in the service area utensil crocks are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -top of black freezer -ice accumulation in black freezer and chest freezers. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026
The prep sink, located in the kitchen, is lacking an air gap. Inspector observed an air break at the time of this inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026

The floors in the kitchen under equipment have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/03/2026

Working containers of dry ingredients and oils are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. PIC labeled during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
Non-food contact surfaces of pots and storage containers have manufacturer's labels on them. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
The wall/floor juncture next to walk-in was observed cracked. Some tiles in kitchen and service area were observed cracked or missing. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2025
